title: epipactis leptochila var. savelliana
intro: Epipactis leptochila var. savelliana, also known as Savelli's Helleborine or Epipactis savelliana, is a rare orchid plant that belongs to the family Orchidaceae and is native to the Mediterranean region. Traditionally, this plant is used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Epipactis leptochila var. savelliana are said to include the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential use in treating skin conditions and wounds. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of this plant are typically used to make infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
